title: Canvas (Agent-to-UI)
description: Create a canvas template, define UI components, have the agent push content to the canvas, and render it inside a messaging channel.

steps.instruction: Create a new canvas template file in the workspace.
steps.action: A file `canvases/weather-card.canvas.json` is created with a template definition that includes a root container, header, body, and footer section.
steps.expectedOutput: `openclaw canvas list` shows `weather-card` with status `template`. The file contains a valid JSON schema with four named slots.

steps.instruction: Define UI components for the canvas: a title, an icon, a data table, and a footer link.
steps.action: The canvas template is updated with component definitions: `title` (text), `icon` (image URL), `dataTable` (key-value pairs), and `footerLink` (action URL).
steps.expectedOutput: `openclaw canvas show weather-card` renders a component tree: `root > header > title, icon`, `root > body > dataTable`, `root > footer > footerLink`. Four components are listed.

steps.instruction: Have the agent push data into the canvas by requesting a weather report.
steps.action: The user sends `Show me the weather for Tokyo` in chat. The agent calls the `canvas:push` tool with the `weather-card` template and fills in the component slots with live data.
steps.expectedOutput: The agent logs `Canvas pushed: weather-card (4 slots filled)`. The canvas data includes Tokyo's temperature, condition icon, and a forecast link.

steps.instruction: Verify that the canvas renders correctly in the Telegram channel as an inline UI card.
steps.action: The Gateway serializes the canvas data into a Telegram-compatible format (using inline keyboard buttons and formatted text) and sends it to the user's chat.
steps.expectedOutput: The Telegram chat displays a formatted card with the weather title, an icon, temperature data in a clean layout, and a clickable footer link. The response is visually distinct from a plain text message.

completionMessage: Congratulations! You created a canvas template, defined reusable components, pushed live data from the agent, and rendered it as a rich UI card in Telegram. Your agent can now deliver structured visual content.
